patch.value:
## Charter
Make workspace scale (item count x agent count) a measured, budgeted property of pm instead of an accident of the current layout. Initiative epic parented under pm-o2kc (storage owns the data-layout physics); indexed in pm-doxj alongside pm-n7rr and pm-usfg.

## Scope
- In: read-path indexing, storage fan-out, bounded-output/cursor pagination, claim+lock throughput, history/git growth policy, and the benchmark harness that gates all of it.
- Out: correctness races at small N (pm-dj98 owns those five open gaps), search relevance/providers (pm-ydp6), token-shape of individual payloads (pm-mpbb lean-output items pm-cfed/pm-g9xk/pm-vxxm — related, not children).

## Delivery order
1. pm-benchmark harness + published baseline (do first — everything else needs numbers).
2. ADR pm-bl8x accepted (index format, fan-out scheme, pagination contract, claim scaling, git growth policy).
3. Persistent read index -> 4. cursor pagination + bounded defaults -> 5. directory fan-out migration -> 6. claim/lock throughput -> 7. history/git growth automation.

## Relationship to the other initiatives
SDK-first (pm-usfg) means the index/pagination primitives land in the SDK and the CLI inherits them; simplification (pm-n7rr) shrinks the surface these guarantees must cover. Scale work must not fork CLI-only fast paths.
Scale-out: sub-second, bounded-token context at 100k-1M items with thousands of concurrent agents

Long-horizon initiative epic (third initiative beside pm-n7rr and pm-usfg) making the workspace scale axis a first-class engineering program: measurement first, then ADR-governed index/fan-out/pagination/claim-scaling work. Duplicate check 2026-07-06: searched scalability/large-workspace/sharding/pagination/index — only unrelated closed items matched; pm-dj98 owns correctness under concurrency, not throughput/scale; pm-ydp6 owns search quality, not workspace-size physics.

Benchmark harness proves: context/next/list/search/get p95 < 1s and default output < 5k tokens on a synthetic 1M-item workspace; 1000-agent parallel claim/update stress runs with zero corruption, zero history drift, bounded retry latency; all changes behind format_version gating with green migration tests; zero feature or behavior regression at small scale
pm context/next/list/search/get stay sub-second and bounded-token at 1M items; claim/mutation throughput supports thousands of parallel agents
Removes the ceiling on workspace size and fleet size: pm becomes usable as the context backbone for the largest projects, which is the stated product goal

Read paths are O(workspace) directory scans and outputs are unbounded; at the stated target scale (hundreds of thousands to millions of items, thousands of agents) every command would be slow and token-catastrophic

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Long-term planning evidence 2026-07-06/07 codex-agent: the scale-out initiative directly covers the user's 100k-1M item / thousands-agent target. Keep the delivery order measurement-first: pm-mi2x benchmark harness, ADR pm-bl8x, derived read index pm-801d, bounded cursor pagination pm-dfg0, storage fan-out, claim/lock throughput pm-lvyd, history/git growth policy, and then federation story pm-is1a. Implementation should land in SDK primitives first so list/search/context/next/get and CLI/MCP inherit the same bounded-output and concurrency guarantees.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
Performance-program map (2026-07-11): where "drastically faster CLI+SDK, zero feature loss" already lives, so nothing gets re-filed.

SHIPPED perf territory (do not re-open): startup strategy ADR pm-irp1 (prebuilt JS + lazy per-command imports) + pm-gt82 esbuild bundling + pm-ss1d ESM-resolution cut (~85ms); extension-activation overhead pm-4oww (~200ms/command, fixed); list full-scan pm-cd2f; CI/test-suite runtime pm-7rlp; warm-read memoization ~2x (PR#421); instant mutations with background semantic refresh; token-cost CI gate pm-cu1i.

OPEN program = this epic's delivery order, unchanged: 1) pm-mi2x harness+baseline (S1-S4 slicing note added today - numbers before optimization), 2) ADR pm-bl8x, 3) pm-801d persistent read index, 4) pm-dfg0 cursor pagination, 5) pm-0qg5 fan-out, 6) pm-lvyd claim/lock throughput, 7) pm-o79o history/git growth. Token-shape (output size, not wall time) is pm-mpbb's lane: pm-cfed lean JSON, pm-g9xk lean errors (pm-vxxm shipped).

Measurement targets S2 should watch beyond the hot paths: settings read on the hot path (zod already off it), per-command JSONL parse volume, git subprocess count per command, MCP long-lived-process advantage vs CLI cold starts.

Principles held: no CLI-only fast paths (index/pagination primitives land in the SDK per pm-usfg, CLI inherits); every optimization needs a before/after from the harness; feature surface is frozen - perf work never drops flags/outputs (contract tests are the guard).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
Performance brainstorm ledger (2026-07-11, maintainer directive: drastically improve pm CLI + pm SDK performance with ZERO feature/functionality loss; documentation-only pass, no code). Companion to the program-map note above — that note fences SHIPPED territory; this one records candidate levers BEYOND the sequenced program so future passes extend instead of re-invent.

THE PLAN IS THE PROGRAM: the sequenced delivery order (pm-mi2x harness -> ADR pm-bl8x -> pm-801d read index -> pm-dfg0 cursor pagination -> pm-0qg5 fan-out -> pm-lvyd claim throughput -> pm-o79o git growth) IS the drastic-improvement plan; the order-of-magnitude wins live in indexed reads + bounded output, not in more startup shaving (startup is near-floor: esbuild bundle pm-gt82 + lazy per-command imports pm-irp1 + ESM cut pm-ss1d + V8 compile cache in src/cli.ts + lazy extension activation pm-5wb6 all shipped). Entry point today: pm-mi2x S1+S2, spike-able with zero deps.

NEW candidate levers — all gated on mi2x before/after numbers (program principle: no optimization without numbers); promote to items only when S3 budgets show they matter:
1. Git-subprocess diet — count and batch/eliminate per-command git spawns (~10-30ms each); pure read paths may need none. Count is already an S2 measurement target.
2. Semantic/BM25 index refresh throughput at scale — background refresh is instant-mutation-safe at 1.7k items, but time-to-fresh after bulk generation at 100k+ items is unmeasured and unowned. Providers/relevance stay pm-ydp6; refresh THROUGHPUT is scale physics and belongs here. S2 measurement lane added on pm-mi2x.
3. Bundle minify — scripts/bundle-cli.mjs ships splitting+sourcemap but no minify; smaller parse/compile + install size. Compile cache already hides repeat-parse cost, so expect a small win — measure, do not assume.
4. Per-phase span attribution — extend the EXISTING OTEL/Sentry span surface (never a new tracing system) so S2 reports attribute p95 to startup/settings/scan/serialize/extension phases; beware pm-25se (OTEL exporter can hold the process alive ~10s).
5. Residual extension overhead — ~150ms with 11 bundled packages (per-extension node_modules symlink resolution + entry.ts type-strip imports) left after the shipped pm-4oww fix; unowned; candidate only if S2 shows it matters at typical package counts.
6. Far/experimental (recorded so nobody re-derives them): V8 startup snapshots (--build-snapshot; hostile to ESM + dynamic import graph); native addon for hot paths (violates simplicity); SQLite/embedded-DB as AUTHORITATIVE store (violates ADR pm-i7i4 — an embedded index may only ever be a gitignored rebuildable cache, which is exactly the pm-801d/pm-bl8x option already on the table).

REJECTED directions (constraints, not oversights): resident daemon/server (ADR pm-i7i4 forbids; the MCP long-lived process is the sanctioned warm path — its advantage over CLI cold starts is an S2 measurement target); CLI-only fast paths (pm-usfg: primitives land in the SDK, CLI/MCP inherit); dropping any flag/output/behavior (surface frozen, contract tests are the guard); token-shape payload work (owned by the pm-mpbb lane: pm-cfed lean JSON, pm-g9xk lean errors).

Framing worth keeping: for agent callers, tokens ARE latency — pm-dfg0 bounded output and the pm-mpbb lean payloads cut end-to-end agent turnaround as much as wall-clock work does; the mi2x token-count metric is a first-class perf metric, not a nice-to-have.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
Ledger extension (2026-07-12 execution session on pm-mi2x; full evidence in mi2x notes): NEW lever 7 discovered by cpu-prof attribution, and a measured correction to lever 1.

LEVER 7 (NEW, measured, biggest single win at small N): lazy-load @sentry/node. The static `import * as SentryModule` in src/core/sentry/instrument.ts costs ~847ms of ESM resolve+compile (the @sentry/node -> OTel -> undici tree from node_modules) on EVERY command — ~60% of pm list wall time at 1.7k items — and is paid even when PM_SENTRY_DISABLED=1 because the opt-out check runs after the module graph loaded. Stub-alias experiment (production layout, sentry aliased to empty module): pm list 1400ms -> 562-801ms, output byte-identical. Fix shape: dynamic `await import("@sentry/node")` inside ensureSentryInit after the isSentryDisabled gate; enabled runs kick init off non-blocking at command start and capture/flush paths await the same promise. Fits ADR pm-irp1 (lazy per-command imports); no ADR conflict.

LEVER 1 CORRECTION (git-subprocess diet): measured ZERO git subprocesses during pm list via PATH shim — pure reads spawn no git at current layout. The lever remains relevant only for mutation/history paths; do not spend read-path effort on it.

LEVER 3 DATA (bundle minify/dep-bundling): bundling all deps EXCEPT sentry = no win (1382-1603ms); bundling everything incl sentry (needs a createRequire banner for @opentelemetry/instrumentation CJS dynamic require) = 890-1005ms. Dep-bundling contradicts accepted ADR pm-irp1 external-deps; with lever 7 landed it buys much less — re-evaluate only with harness numbers if lever 7 is insufficient.

Startup floor re-confirmed at ~90ms (--version fast path == bare node). Remaining post-lever-7 budget (~500-650ms at 1.7k items) is genuine scan/serialize work — owned by the sequenced program (pm-801d index, pm-dfg0 bounded output).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: 2026-07-12 implementation baseline/update: before optimization on the real ~1.7k tracker, warm CLI list/get/next/context/search were ~1.4/1.2/1.7/1.8/2.0s and a prior 100k baseline was 28-32s. The broad performance tranche now ships a local scale harness, lazy Sentry loading, versioned directory-signature metadata-index fast path, and SDK-first cursor/bounded-output primitives. Isolated 100k result: CLI list/get/next/context/search/create/claim 1855/1325/3470/3116/2941/723/692ms; SDK 923/440/2563/2587/2591/8/7ms; all payloads under 5000 estimated tokens. Full 1M/sub-second/transactional index work remains on pm-801d.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Local final-gate evidence for performance tranche commit 4dd7eb5f: build, typecheck, docs/skills, static quality, token budget, 277 test files / 5587 tests, coverage 100% statements/branches/functions/lines, version policy, secret scan, npx packed smoke, 88-command package-first dogfood, npm pack, compatibility, and live Sentry/telemetry all passed locally. pnpm lint also passed with zero ESLint warnings and zero jscpd clones. The local Greptile wrapper was rerun after commit and timed out after 600000ms with a documented skip and no findings. No GitHub Actions runner was used.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: PR #525 first review loop: 10 inline threads inspected, reacted to, replied to, and resolved. Accepted fixes: benchmark spawn cleanup, one-pass scale generation, cursor runtime validation, mutation-tolerant positional fallback, presentation-independent fingerprints, and null-safe cache access. Declined with evidence: direct tracker-file edits are outside ADR pm-bl8x and require reindex; semantic paging cannot expand beyond stable search.max_results without reordering hybrid scores. Verification after fixes: 5588 tests, 100/100/100/100, lint/static/type/contracts, local 10k benchmark, package-first dogfood, and packed consumer all green.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: PR #525 second review loop: 9 new bot inline comments plus edited summaries inspected, reacted to, individually replied to, and all 19 cumulative threads resolved. New P1 arbitrary-directory deletion fixed with fixture-manifest guard. Additional fixes: context scale constant/fingerprint completeness, zero-limit cursor consistency, context tool --after contract, search format exclusion, explicit direct-edit recovery test. Local evidence: lint/static/type/contracts, 10k benchmark, 277 files/5588 tests at 100/100/100/100.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Frontier update 2026-07-12 (post PR#525, pass #74): the broad perf tranche SHIPPED — pm-mi2x scale harness (10k/100k/1M generator + benchmark:scale* + deterministic 10k regression gate), pm-dfg0 cursor pagination (SDK pagination primitives; --after/has_more/next_cursor/applied_limit/truncated across list/search/context; next queues bounded), pm-1ybs lazy Sentry, and ADR pm-bl8x ACCEPTED (versioned rebuildable derived index CACHE_VERSION 7, hash-bucket migration contract, opaque query-bound cursors, per-item lock scaling, Git/history growth policy). Measured 100k: list 1.9s CLI / 0.9s SDK, next 3.5s/2.6s — down from 28-32s baseline; all payloads <5k tokens. Remaining epic frontier = pm-801d ONLY (1M/sub-second/transactional index target, explicitly left open by the PR) — pm next now recommends it. The mi2x-gated candidate-lever ledger (6 levers, note on this item) is now UNGATED: harness exists, levers can be measured before adoption. Downstream unblock: pm-55ra (both blockers dfg0+h3no closed) is actionable; pm-3hps still waits on 801d.

patch.value: Frontier update 2026-07-12 (post PR#525, pass #74): the broad perf tranche SHIPPED — pm-mi2x scale harness (10k/100k/1M generator + benchmark:scale* + deterministic 10k regression gate), pm-dfg0 cursor pagination (SDK pagination primitives; --after/has_more/next_cursor/applied_limit/truncated across list/search/context; next queues bounded), pm-1ybs lazy Sentry, and ADR pm-bl8x ACCEPTED (versioned rebuildable derived index CACHE_VERSION 7, hash-bucket migration contract, opaque query-bound cursors, per-item lock scaling, Git/history growth policy). Measured 100k: list 1.9s CLI / 0.9s SDK, next 3.5s/2.6s — down from 28-32s baseline; all payloads <5k tokens. Remaining sequenced program: pm-801d derived read index (the 1M/sub-second/transactional target, explicitly left open by the PR; pm next now recommends it) -> pm-0qg5 fan-out -> pm-lvyd claim/lock throughput -> pm-o79o history/git growth — all four still open. The mi2x-gated candidate-lever ledger (levers in the two 2026-07-11 notes + lever 7 in the 07-12 note) is now UNGATED: harness exists, levers can be measured before adoption. Downstream unblock: pm-55ra (both blockers dfg0+h3no closed) is actionable; pm-3hps still waits on 801d.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Scale review 2026-07-13: the shipped 100k-item baseline and cursor/benchmark foundations remain the evidence base. pm-801d is now actionable because pm-bl8x and pm-mi2x are closed; it should precede pm-3hps, whose remaining effective blocker is pm-801d. Preserve dual targets: small repositories must remain near-instant and understandable, while million-item and multi-agent workloads require bounded scans, resumable cursors, incremental indexes, deterministic ranking, backpressure, observability, and benchmarked token budgets.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Scale relationship organization 2026-07-13: million-item agent story pm-g2zz now explicitly depends on derived read index pm-801d and thousand-agent claim throughput pm-lvyd. Existing thematic relations preserve its context-engine and evaluation lineage without adding order edges that current issue pm-6irg would misclassify into cycles. Graph analytics pm-7ob5 and conformance pm-m2il reuse the scale harness pm-mi2x and require deterministic bounded-memory, latency, token, incremental-update, and one-million-item evidence.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Latency spot-measurement 2026-07-17 (warm store, main @ c6d82b58, global install): get 677-710ms, list --status open 664ms, graph audit --summary 658ms (FIRST measurement of the PR#580 surface: full 1859-node/3071-edge scan adds ~0 over process floor — analytics are not the bottleneck, startup remains it), context 882ms, search 949ms, next 1097ms (slowest single surface; candidate for lever attention when 801d lands). Confirms the standing profile: per-command cost is ESM-resolution dominated; graph analytics inherit the floor. No new items — lever ledger here owns all directions.

value.author: codex-ecosystem-planner
value.text: Scale-governance review 2026-07-18: current all-status corpus is 1,866 items, 30,491 history events, 35.2 MB of history streams, 3,108 graph edges, and 73 root command names. The synthetic 10k/100k/1M harness and cursor bounds are shipped; the remaining physical critical path is still pm-801d derived read index -> pm-3hps signal store, with pm-0qg5 fan-out, pm-lvyd thousand-agent claim throughput, and pm-o79o history/git growth policy following. Long-duration context is separately owned by pm-5qmm and pm-bwyw; arbitrary indexed composition by pm-plwm. Required scale matrix: tiny 3-item seconds-old project, current real corpus, 10k, 100k, 1M, high-churn thousand-agent contention, and decade-shaped archive. Gate p50/p95/p99 latency, peak RSS, index rebuild/recovery, write amplification, stale-read bounds, token count, ranking quality, and correctness under crash/replay; never trade away behavior or authoritative JSONL recoverability.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Latency spot-check 2026-07-19 (1,886 nodes / 3,171 edges, warm, local): pm --version 54-84ms (startup lever holding post irp1/gt82); pm get <id> ~654ms; pm list --limit 5 ~685ms; pm graph audit ~677ms; pm next 829-873ms. Roughly 600-800ms of per-command WORK dominates every read - startup is no longer the bottleneck, workspace scan/assembly is. Consistent with the existing lever order (801d substrate entry next). Single-item pm get paying ~600ms suggests full-workspace projection on the read path is the highest-value target for the instant-feel mandate.

patch.value: Latency spot-check 2026-07-19 (1,886 nodes / 3,171 edges, warm, local): pm --version 54-84ms (fast path - bypasses the command module graph); pm get <id> ~654ms; pm list --limit 5 ~685ms; pm graph audit ~677ms; pm next 829-873ms. get/list/graph-audit cluster at ~650-685ms = the shared per-command floor, consistent with the 2026-07-17 finding that ESM resolution dominates and analytics inherit the floor; pm next pays ~+150-200ms of real selection work on top. No regression vs 07-17 (get 677-710, next 1097 then - next actually improved). Lever order unchanged: 801d derived read index remains the substrate for the instant-feel mandate; the ESM floor itself is lever-7/irp1 territory (already shipped for Sentry - remaining floor is the command graph).

value.author: codex-tracker-governance
value.text: Scale strategy refresh 2026-07-21: the live corpus is 1,901 items and 31,782 pre-mutation history entries, so repository health at this size is evidence, not proof of the 100k-1M target. The existing sequence remains correct: benchmark and workload generators first, persistent derived read index second, cursor and feature-store reads third, adaptive context packing and memory tiers fourth, then thousand-agent claim/merge/federation stress. Acceptance must remain bounded by latency, memory, tokens, deterministic continuation, merge correctness, and small-project parity rather than raw throughput alone.
